CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT, value1 VARCHAR(10), value2 VARCHAR(10));
SELECT id FROM test_table WHERE value1 >= value2;
SELECT id FROM test_table WHERE value1 >= 'ab';
SELECT id FROM test_table WHERE 'aa' >= value2;
SELECT id FROM test_table WHERE value1 >= value2 AND id > 1;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT id FROM test_table WHERE value1 >= value2) AS subquery;
CREATE TABLE second_table (key_id INT, description VARCHAR(20));
SELECT t1.id, t2.description FROM test_table t1 JOIN second_table t2 ON t1.id >= t2.key_id;
SELECT t1.id FROM test_table t1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT * FROM second_table t2 WHERE t1.value1 >= t2.description);
SELECT MAX(t1.id) FROM test_table t1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT * FROM second_table t2 WHERE t1.value1 >= t2.description);
